Package de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.op

Interface Operator

All Superinterfaces:
EqualsModProofIrrelevancy, Named, SVSubstitute
All Known Subinterfaces:
IObserverFunction, IProgramMethod, IProgramVariable, ParsableVariable, ProgramConstruct, QuantifiableVariable, SchemaVariable, SortedOperator, TermTransformer, UpdateableOperator
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractSortedOperator, AbstractSV, AbstractTermTransformer, AddCast, ArrayBaseInstanceOf, ConstantValue, CreateBeforeLoopUpdate, CreateFrameCond, CreateHeapAnonUpdate, CreateLocalAnonUpdate, CreateWellformedCond, DivideLCRMonomials, DivideMonomials, ElementaryUpdate, EnumConstantValue, Equality, ExpandQueriesMetaConstruct, FormulaSV, Function, IfExThenElse, IfThenElse, IntroAtPreDefsOp, Junctor, LocationVariable, LogicVariable, MemberPVToField, MetaAdd, MetaArithBitMaskOp, MetaBinaryAnd, MetaBinaryOr, MetaBinaryXOr, MetaDiv, MetaEqual, MetaGeq, MetaGreater, MetaLeq, MetaLess, MetaMul, MetaPow, MetaShift, MetaShiftLeft, MetaShiftRight, MetaSub, Metavariable, Modality, ModalOperatorSV, ObserverEqualityMetaConstruct, ObserverFunction, ProgramConstant, ProgramMethod, ProgramSV, ProgramVariable, Quantifier, SkolemTermSV, SortDependingFunction, SubstOp, TermLabelSV, TermSV, Transformer, UpdateApplication, UpdateJunctor, UpdateSV, VariableSV, WarySubstOp
public interface Operator extends Named, SVSubstitute, EqualsModProofIrrelevancy
All symbols acting as members of a term e.g. logical operators, predicates, functions, variables etc. have to implement this interface.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    int
    arity()
    the arity of this operator
    boolean
    bindVarsAt(int n)
    Tells whether the operator binds variables at the n-th subterm.
    default boolean
    equalsModProofIrrelevancy(Object obj)
    Checks whether this object and the other object represent the same data, whilst ignoring attributes that are not relevant for the purpose of these objects in the proof.
    default int
    hashCodeModProofIrrelevancy()
    Compute a hashcode that represents the proof-relevant fields of this object.
    boolean
    isRigid()
    Tells whether the operator is rigid.
    Sort
    sort(ImmutableArray<Term> terms)
    Determines the sort of the Term if it would be created using this Operator as top level operator and the given terms as sub terms.
    default boolean
    validTopLevel(Term term)
     
    void
    validTopLevelException(Term term)
    Checks whether the top level structure of the given @link Term is syntactically valid, given the assumption that the top level operator of the term is the same as this Operator.

    Methods inherited from interface de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.Named

    name

  • Method Details

    • arity

      int arity()
      the arity of this operator

    • sort

      Sort sort(ImmutableArray<Term> terms)
      Determines the sort of the Term if it would be created using this Operator as top level operator and the given terms as sub terms. The assumption that the constructed term would be allowed is not checked.
      Parameters:
      terms - an array of Term containing the subterms of a (potential) term with this operator as top level operator
      Returns:
      sort of the term with this operator as top level operator of the given substerms

    • bindVarsAt

      boolean bindVarsAt(int n)
      Tells whether the operator binds variables at the n-th subterm.

    • isRigid

      boolean isRigid()
      Tells whether the operator is rigid.

    • validTopLevelException

      void validTopLevelException(Term term) throws TermCreationException
      Checks whether the top level structure of the given @link Term is syntactically valid, given the assumption that the top level operator of the term is the same as this Operator. The assumption that the top level operator and the term are equal is NOT checked.
      Throws:
      TermCreationException - if a construction error was recognise

    • validTopLevel

      default boolean validTopLevel(Term term)
      Returns:
      true iff the top level structure of the Term is valid, i.e. its parameters and types are correct.

    • equalsModProofIrrelevancy

      default boolean equalsModProofIrrelevancy(Object obj)
      Description copied from interface: EqualsModProofIrrelevancy
      Checks whether this object and the other object represent the same data, whilst ignoring attributes that are not relevant for the purpose of these objects in the proof.
      Specified by:
      equalsModProofIrrelevancy in interface EqualsModProofIrrelevancy
      Parameters:
      obj - other object
      Returns:
      whether these objects are equal

    • hashCodeModProofIrrelevancy

      default int hashCodeModProofIrrelevancy()
      Description copied from interface: EqualsModProofIrrelevancy
      Compute a hashcode that represents the proof-relevant fields of this object.
      Specified by:
      hashCodeModProofIrrelevancy in interface EqualsModProofIrrelevancy
      Returns:
      the hashcode
